metadata.projectName: GutWise™ Canonical Integration & Connector Architecture — RCM Assessment
metadata.initiatedBy: RCM /optimo-rcm command — review of Collin King's GUT-ARCH-004 Slack ideation

objectives.statement: Define canonical integration architecture ensuring GutWise remains vendor-independent with best-in-class integrations
objectives.rcmAssessment: Achieved in principle but underspecified in practice. The three design principles are philosophically sound. However, the connector abstraction lacks canonical data models — without them, true vendor independence is aspirational rather than architectural.

objectives.statement: Ensure no external vendor owns GutWise clinical logic
objectives.rcmAssessment: STRONG — Evaluator score 4.25/5.0. This is the most defensible element of the architecture. Add formal architectural enforcement (no-clinical-sprawl gate) rather than relying on principle alone.

objectives.statement: Provider-as-gatekeeper: AI generates, provider reviews/edits/approves, connectors execute
objectives.rcmAssessment: Sound safety architecture (3.55/5.0) but critically dependent on provider vigilance. Automation bias risk is real. Need audit proving meaningful review, not rubber-stamping.

objectives.statement: Expose GutWise clinical intelligence via MCP layer for external AI agents
objectives.rcmAssessment: Highest-risk architectural decision (2.60/5.0). MCP protocol immaturity + external AI agent access to clinical PHI = significant security surface area. Should be decoupled: clinical service API with MCP as one frontend, not the only frontend.

concepts.name: Digital Twin Connector for Protocol Simulation
Clinical Reasoning Audit Chain as Regulatory Connector
GutWise as White-Label Clinical AI Engine

research.question: What is the regulatory boundary where bidirectional closed-loop control (wearable biofeedback) crosses from CDSS into active medical device territory?
What are the connector failure modes in healthcare integration (stale OAuth tokens, lab backlog timeouts, formulary changes mid-protocol) and what reliability guarantees are needed?
What is the MCP threat model for prompt injection originating from connector webhook payloads propagating through MCP layer to AI clinical reasoning?
Can the connector certification suite be codified into an open standard analogous to FHIR but for operational integration?

evidence.finding: GutWise MVP is live at gutwise-journey.lovable.app with hardening 13/14 complete
No connector implementations exist in the current codebase — this architecture defines what needs to be built
Fullscript provides OAuth 2.0 + REST + Webhooks + JavaScript SDK for supplement fulfillment
MCP protocol is at v1.0 (2025) with TypeScript SDK available but limited healthcare deployment precedents
FDA CDSS enforcement discretion applies only when provider independently reviews the basis of recommendations
evidence.source: FDA Guidance on Clinical Decision Support Software (2022)

assumptions.assumption: Provider review gate is sufficient safeguard against AI clinical errors
assumptions.ifFalse: Automation bias + provider fatigue = illusory gate; unsafe recommendations reach patients
assumptions.validation: Clinical simulation study with injected unsafe recommendations; target >99% rejection rate

assumptions.assumption: Vendor APIs remain available and backward-compatible
assumptions.ifFalse: Breaking API change during active treatment cycle causes data corruption or failed fulfillment
assumptions.validation: Contract testing in CI/CD; synthetic monitoring every 15 minutes

assumptions.assumption: MCP protocol security is sufficient for clinical PHI access
assumptions.ifFalse: External AI agents can exfiltrate patient data through MCP tool calls; no fine-grained access control
assumptions.validation: Third-party penetration test targeting MCP endpoints before any external agent connection

assumptions.assumption: De-identified clinical data meets HIPAA Safe Harbor or expert determination standards
assumptions.ifFalse: Model inversion attacks re-identify patients from model outputs — HIPAA breach for all training data patients
assumptions.validation: Engage qualified statistician for HIPAA expert determination; adversarial re-identification testing

decisions.decision: Prioritize canonical data model definition before connector implementation
decisions.rationale: Evaluator identifies the connector abstraction as operationally defined but semantically absent. Building connectors without canonical data models guarantees vendor lock-in through data model coupling. Cost: 4-8 week delay. Benefit: abstraction proven against hardest integration (labs) before investment.
decisions.tradeoffs: Delays Fullscript connector delivery by 4-8 weeks; prevents building on a leaky abstraction that would require costly refactoring later.

decisions.decision: Decouple MCP from being the sole AI integration surface
decisions.rationale: MCP protocol immaturity + single-point-of-failure + external AI agent PHI access = highest-risk architectural element (Evaluator 2.60, Threat R1 P×I=1.50). Clinical service API with MCP as one of multiple frontends is more robust.
decisions.tradeoffs: Adds architectural complexity (API gateway + MCP adapter vs direct MCP); buys protocol independence and security surface reduction.

decisions.decision: Adopt hybrid vendor strategy: deep partnerships with 2-3 anchor vendors + generic connector abstraction for the rest
decisions.rationale: Social perspective identifies that pure 'swappable connector' philosophy alienates vendors whose cooperation depth is essential. Fullscript and PatientNow need partnership depth; future grocery/pharmacy connectors benefit from generic abstraction.
decisions.tradeoffs: Compromises pure architectural orthodoxy for practical adoption reality. Anchor vendors get deeper integration that may create some coupling.

decisions.rationale: Executor plan is dependency-aware and each phase delivers standalone value. Phase 1 (Connector Framework) + Phase 2 (Fullscript) deliver the highest-value integration fastest.
decisions.tradeoffs: 18-26 weeks is a significant build commitment. Could be accelerated by reducing Phase 4 (PatientNow) scope to unidirectional sync only.

dependencies.name: Fullscript Partner API access
dependencies.status: sandbox available immediately; production requires approval

dependencies.name: PatientNow API credentials
dependencies.status: requires contractual agreement; begin procurement during Phase 1

risks.description: Missing HIPAA BAAs with connector sub-processors
Inadequate audit logging — HIPAA requires 6+ year retention of all ePHI access records
MCP server exposes clinical PHI to external AI agents without per-scope access controls
Connector outage cascades cause silent clinical data loss or incorrect treatment
FDA CDSS device classification — if AI recommendations constitute 'device' functions requiring 510(k)
Provider approval gate bypass via race condition or state desynchronization
API contract breaking changes from Fullscript/PatientNow causing connector failures
Vendor lock-in through connector decay — vendors restrict API access after GutWise investment

constraints.description: HIPAA Security Rule: all ePHI access must be logged, audit records retained 6+ years
FDA CDSS: AI must never be the final decision-maker; provider approval gate must be non-bypassable
CLIA: Lab results must be provider-reviewed before patient portal release
HIPAA minimum necessary: each connector must limit PHI transmission to minimum required
MCP external agents must have scoped, revocable access — no blanket PHI access
Provider adoption requires explainable AI — black-box recommendations erode trust
State telemedicine laws vary; GutWise must apply jurisdictional rules per patient-provider interaction

successCriteria.rationale: Below 10% = rubber-stamping (regulatory risk). Above 40% = AI quality insufficient.

successCriteria.metric: Time saved per new patient workup
successCriteria.rationale: Core value prop for practitioners — must save meaningful time
Relationship-driven market; word-of-mouth is primary growth channel
Clinical outcome proxy — platform delivers on healing promise
Validates connector abstraction; one connector = fancy API wrapper, not an architecture

successCriteria.threshold: HIPAA assessment + FDA CDSS opinion completed before GA
successCriteria.rationale: No practice or health system will adopt without documented compliance

feedback.condition: Must demonstrate time savings and maintain clinical autonomy
Published validation studies, transparent AI scoring, documented enforcement discretion analysis
Incremental order volume; preferred integration depth in exchange for partnership
Must retain primacy as legal medical record; co-marketing opportunity
SOC 2 Type II minimum; HITRUST preferred; documented data flow diagrams; penetration test results

specifications.name: Connector boundary contract (pre-build prerequisite)
specifications.description: Formal, versioned, testable specification that connector developers implement against without accessing clinical engine source. Must include: REST/AsyncAPI specs, failure mode contracts (connector down for 30 min? 24 hours?), latency budgets, data freshness SLAs, conflict resolution semantics.

specifications.name: Canonical data models per connector type (pre-build prerequisite)
specifications.description: Semantic models for supplement fulfillment, EHR/EMR, and diagnostic labs using existing standards (LOINC, SNOMED, FHIR, NDC). Without these, the connector abstraction is operational-only and will leak vendor semantics.

specifications.name: MCP authorization scoping
specifications.description: Per-agent, per-tool, per-clinic access control. Clinical reasoning agent: read-only connector data. Operational agent: write to Fullscript but not clinical engine. Provider agent: approve but not modify lab results. Enforce at Clinical Service Layer, not just MCP transport.

specifications.name: Immutable approval snapshot with cryptographic hash
specifications.description: When provider clicks Approve, capture hash of entire treatment plan. Connector layer verifies hash before external sync. Prevents race condition bypass of approval gate.
